node.statement: (Finite-dimensionality of commutant). From Node 1.3, if φ is extreme in C under m constraints, then dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) ≤ m + 1 < ∞. Since π_φ(A)'^{sa} is the self-adjoint part of the *-algebra π_φ(A)', and every element of π_φ(A)' decomposes as T = Re(T) + i·Im(T) with Re(T), Im(T) ∈ π_φ(A)'^{sa}, we have dim_C(π_φ(A)') = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) ≤ m + 1 < ∞. Thus π_φ(A)' is a finite-dimensional von Neumann algebra (it is a *-subalgebra of B(H_φ) closed in all operator topologies, being finite-dimensional).
